lastReleaseMessage: 100-polish pass: mobile/iOS meta, save indicator, keyboard shortcuts (?, Esc, Ctrl+K/S, Alt+1-4), help dialog, accordion polish, Layout Designer micro-interactions, library tile polish, color popover a11y, auto-grow textareas, visibility-change persist, responsive breakpoints down to 380px, hover-none/reduced-motion/high-contrast support

barRaise.topFinding: The five-day stall broke: v0.16.0 (de4fe25, 2026-06-06) landed the 100-item polish pass and committed the entire uncommitted pile in one go (six backlogged report docs, the 06-03 drift-fixes, the spec/CLAUDE micro-edits, the 15-script sweep), so the working tree is clean and the verdict rises one tier from stalled to at-risk. But two HIGHs are still open and unchanged. The dashboard-clobber gun fired exactly as predicted: the 06-06 bar-raise wrote barRaise into YaA.json, the release ran write-dashboard-status.ps1, and barRaise, audit.findings, and workQueueDepth are null/0-0-0/0 again. The work-queue still holds 18 pending yaa/apo items, oldest now 20 days. The same commit did the .bak cleanup backwards, committing all 15 backup files as tracked clutter and the UTF-8 BOM onto 15 scripts. The label engine itself is healthy; the whole risk surface is the dashboard-writeback / work-queue / docs perimeter.
